center.name: 中东
center.summary: 中东是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联20种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、大红鹳、Yellow-legged gull等20种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: __NOTOC__ The Armenian gull (Larus armenicus) is a large gull found in the Caucasus and the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blackstart (Oenanthe melanura) is a chat found in desert regions in North Africa, the Middle East and the Arabian Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Oenanthe lugens lugens - MHNT The mourning wheatear (Oenanthe lugens) is a bird, one of 14 species of wheatear found in northern Africa and the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pale rockfinch or pale rock sparrow (Carpospiza brachydactyla) is a small sparrow found in the Middle East and Central Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in North Africa and the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Sand partridges are endemic across the Middle East. Populations are distributed across Eastern Egypt, Eastern Sudan, Israel, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The four subspecies of the sand partridge are native to specific parts of the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Tristram's starling calls Tristram's starling (Onychognathus tristramii), also known as Dead Sea starling or Tristram's grackle, is a species of starling native to the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-legged gull or western yellow-legged gull (Larus michahellis) is a large gull found in Europe, the Middle East and North Africa, which has only recently achieved wide recognition as a distinct species

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Common in the Old World, they are found in Northern (coastal) and Sub-Saharan Africa, the Indian subcontinent (south of the Himalayas), the Middle East, the Levant, the Persian Gulf, the Gulf of Aden, the Red Sea, and the Mediterranean countries of Southern Europe

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|upright|By a nest in a saguaro cactus in [[Arizona, U.S.]] thumb|House sparrows perching on a roof, during winter in the Southern Alps (New Zealand)|Southern Alps of New Zealand The house sparrow originated in the Middle East and spread, along with agriculture, to most of Eurasia and parts of North Africa. Since the mid-19th century, it has reached most of the world, chiefly due to deliberate introductions, but also through natural and shipborne dispersal. Its introduced range encompasses most of North America (including Bermuda),{{cite web |url=https://audubon.bm/bird/house-sparrow |title=House Sparrow: Passer domesticus |author= |date= |website=Bermuda Audubon Society |publisher=Bermuda Audubon Society |access-date=2022-07-04 |quote=Status: Abundant naturalized species

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Many European birds are resident, but some northern populations and most Asian breeders are migratory, wintering in northern Africa, the Middle East or India

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Found in parts of the Middle East and sub-Saharan Africa, it is also known as the northern orange-tufted sunbird

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The woodchat shrike breeds in southern Europe, the Middle East and northwest Africa, and winters in tropical Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Scandinavian and Russian birds start moving south from mid-September onwards, most birds wintering in Europe, western Turkey and the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cinclus | synonyms = Sturnus cinclus }} The white-throated dipper (Cinclus cinclus), also known as the European dipper or just dipper, is an aquatic, passerine bird found in Europe, the Middle East, Central Asia, and the Indian subcontinent

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | title = The Birds of the Western Palearctic concise edition (2 volumes) | publisher = Oxford University Press |year = 1998| location =Oxford |pages = 1103–1106| isbn = 978-0-19-854099-1 }} and Asiatic birds move to the Middle East, India, and Southeast Asia. Birds from the North American population also winter in tropical Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This subspecies also ranges in much of Asia Minor, mainly Turkey, spottily through the Middle East and the Arabian Peninsula into northern Yemen and Oman to its eastern limits throughout the Caucasus,{{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20170209005309/http://www.abcc-am.org/golden-eagle.html Golden Eagle in Armenia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Running Press, Philadelphia. With a total range in Europe and Asia of about and a total population estimated to be between 100,000 and 500,000 individuals, the IUCN lists the bird's conservation status as being of least concern, although the trend is listed as decreasing. The vast majority of eagle-owls live in Continental Europe, Scandinavia, Russia (which is almost certainly where the peak numbers and diversity of race occurs), and Central Asia. Additional minor populations exist in Anatolia, the northern Middle East, the montane upper part of South Asia, China, Korea and in Japan; in addition, an estimated 12 to 40 pairs are thought to reside in the United Kingdom as of 2016 (where they are arguably non-native), a number which may be on the rise, and have successfully bred in the UK since at least 1996. Tame eagle-owls have occasionally been used in pest control because of their size to deter large birds such as gulls from nesting

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is a fully migratory bird, breeding in Kazakhstan and wintering in the Middle East, Indian subcontinent, and Sudan with a very small wintering population in Iberia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is also native to parts of Asia, including Korea, Japan and China, and to the Middle East, including Iran and Kazakhstan
